name: William Carlos Williams
abstract: William Carlos Williams (September 17, 1883 – March 4, 1963) was an American-Puerto Rican poet and physician closely associated with modernism and imagism. His Spring and All (1923) was written in the wake of  T.

about_quotes.speaker: A.B. Spellman
about_quotes.evidence: it is not giving you specific insight in that sense of William Carlos Williams’ famous poem about how poetry — how you cannot learn the news from reading a poem but yet men die every day from lack of it.
